one_line_thesis: Agilent is a life-science tools compounder with durable CrossLab consumables on installed instruments; at ~$134 the synthesis IRR is 4.19% per year, below our 15% hold bar, so we watch for a better entry or faster biopharma recovery.

deep_dive.executive_summary: Agilent is a life-science tools compounder with a razor-and-blade model across three reorganized segments. FY2025 free cash flow per share is about $4.04 (cash from operations $1.56 billion less capital spending $407 million, divided by roughly 285 million diluted shares). H1 FY2026 shows $3.63 billion of revenue (+8.5% year over year) and diluted earnings per share of $2.27 versus $1.86 in the prior-year period. At about $134 per share (NYSE close 2026-07-09), our base case implies 4.19% per year over seven years, below the 15% hold band. Bear and bull scenarios span -1.8% to 7.7% per year...

decision.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

valuation.interpretation: value_per_share is withheld while any material additive component is unpriced; priced_components_per_share is not a complete security value.

scenario_contract.rule: Cases must change cited causal operating, capital, probability, timing, or financing drivers—not only a terminal multiple.

change_control.rule: A source fact is locked. Every assumption change requires a reason, author, timestamp, and before/after value.

optionality.rule: Every option requires a beneficiary, probability, timing, remaining capital, failure case, and non-overlap control; absence of option value must be explicit rather than unvalued.

committee.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

method_fit.label: High-return compounder
applicability_tests: Owner earnings can be separated from stock compensation, working capital, and maintenance capital.
Incremental returns on retained capital are observable and durable.
The competitive advantage explains pricing, retention, share, or cost outcomes.
Terminal value is supported by a reinvestment runway rather than an unexplained multiple.
failure_modes: growth projected without its capital cost
terminal value unsupported by a durable mechanism

validation_cohort.purpose: Test depletion, commodity exposure, finite reserves, and royalty-unit reconciliation.
Test tangible capital, credit losses, funding, regulation, and excess-capital realization.
Test owner earnings, capital-light reinvestment, cyclicality, and buyback value creation.
Test normalized margins, industry supply response, replacement cost, and reinvestment timing.
Test product concentration, pipeline probabilities, patent cliffs, and catalyst timing.

method_fit.rule: Use one primary method and no more than two corroborating methods; irrelevant methods and personas remain silent.

outcomes.weighting_rule: Do not change a persona's relevance within a power zone until at least 20 attributable, dividend-aware outcomes exist there; never reweight automatically.

attribution.explanation: A complete security value is intentionally withheld while material components are unpriced. Attribution restarts after the first proof-complete baseline.

total_return_panel.error: Vendor events are discovery evidence; primary-source completeness has not been established.
total_return_panel.return_contract: split-adjusted close excluding dividends; event amounts normalized for later splits; cash reinvested at ex-date close; pre-tax nominal

coverage.note: Vendor events are discovery evidence; primary-source completeness has not been established.

timeline.label: H1 FY2026 revenue $3.63B (+8.5% YoY) and diluted EPS $2.27 signal continued recovery after biopharma softness
FY2025 net revenue $6.95B (+6.7%); operating income $1.48B; free cash flow ~$1.15B after $407M capex
Segment reorganization into Life Sciences and Diagnostics Markets, Agilent CrossLab, and Applied Markets; historical segment data recast
Effective Nov 1, 2024 operating model shift: consumables/chemistries moved to CrossLab; instruments split LS&D vs Applied Markets
Closed $1.2B senior notes offering (2027 and 2034) to partially fund BIOVECTRA acquisition and term-loan prepayment
Agreed to acquire BIOVECTRA CDMO for $925M from H.I.G. Capital; closed before calendar 2025
FY2023 revenue $6.83B peak before biopharma funding downturn pressured instrument placements
Acquired Resolution Bioscience to expand cancer diagnostics and NGS portfolio in diagnostics group
Spun off electronic measurement business as Keysight Technologies; Agilent focused on life sciences, diagnostics, and applied chemical analysis
IPO spin-off from Hewlett-Packard as independent Agilent Technologies

industry.structure: Global life-science tools market is oligopolistic among Agilent, Danaher, Thermo Fisher, and Waters; switching costs arise from validated lab workflows and recurring consumables on installed instruments.
industry.share_shift: Agilent gained share in LS&D (+11% FY2025 including BIOVECTRA) while Applied Markets grew only 1%; peers with broader bioprocessing exposure recovered faster from the 2023–2024 funding downturn.
industry.trend: Biopharma capital budgets are stabilizing in 2025–2026; CDMO/consolidation (BIOVECTRA) and CrossLab services mix shift revenue toward recurring streams; China and academic funding remain swing factors.

top_claims.claim: Leadership / governance on watch. 2 executive sales ($99,981) in 120d
EPS (basic) growth moved from -3.6% to +60.0% YoY in the latest quarter. Source: SEC XBRL quarterly filings.
Core operating metrics (operating income, revenues) are accelerating together — growth moved from -6.1% to +33.0% YoY in the latest quarter. Source: SEC XBRL quarterly filings.

risk_claims.claim: *Primary risk: A prolonged biopharma and academic funding downturn reduces instrument placements and operating leverage, forcing margin compression despite consumables stability.
China and academic budget cuts persist two or more years (invert pillar 2).
BIOVECTRA integration under-delivers on revenue or margin (acquisition was ~$925M, financed partly with new debt).
